mission.title: Install llm-d Workload Variant Autoscaler (WVA)
mission.description: Deploy the Workload Variant Autoscaler (WVA) for dynamic autoscaling of llm-d inference deployments. WVA adjusts replica counts based on inference server saturation, optimizing resource utilization and reducing tail latency. Supports full installation (llm-d stack + WVA) or WVA-only mode connecting to an existing deployment. Requires Prometheus with HTTPS.

steps.title: Prepare prerequisites
steps.description:
Ensure you have:
1. Kubernetes cluster (v1.31+) with GPU support
2. Gateway control plane deployed (Istio)
3. Prometheus monitoring stack with HTTPS support

**Platform-specific Prometheus setup:**
- **OpenShift:** Enable User Workload Monitoring
- **GKE:** Install in-cluster Prometheus (GMP lacks HTTP API)
- **Kind/Minikube:** Install Prometheus with TLS configuration

See [monitoring documentation](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/docs/monitoring) for details.

steps.title: Create namespace and HuggingFace token
steps.description:
```bash
export NAMESPACE=llm-d-autoscaler
kubectl create namespace ${NAMESPACE}

kubectl create secret generic llm-d-hf-token \
  --from-literal=HF_TOKEN=<your-hf-token> \
  -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**For OpenShift only:**
```bash
kubectl label namespace ${NAMESPACE} openshift.io/user-monitoring=true --overwrite
```

steps.title: Configure WVA values
steps.description:
Edit `workload-autoscaling/values.yaml` with platform-specific settings:

**OpenShift:**
```yaml
wva:
  prometheus:
    monitoringNamespace: openshift-user-workload-monitoring
    baseURL: "https://thanos-querier.openshift-monitoring.svc.cluster.local:9091"
    tls:
      insecureSkipVerify: true
```

**GKE:**
```yaml
wva:
  prometheus:
    monitoringNamespace: llm-d-monitoring
    baseURL: "http://llmd-kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus.llm-d-monitoring.svc.cluster.local:9090"
```

**Accelerator type:**
```yaml
va:
  accelerator: L40S  # or A100, H100, Intel-Max-1550
```

steps.title: Install WVA CRDs
steps.description:
Install the VariantAutoscaling CRD before deploying:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d-incubation/workload-variant-autoscaler/v0.5.0/charts/workload-variant-autoscaler/crds/llmd.ai_variantautoscalings.yaml
kubectl get crd variantautoscalings.llmd.ai
```

steps.title: Install WVA with llm-d stack
steps.description:
**Option A: Full installation** (llm-d stack + WVA):
```bash
cd guides/workload-autoscaling
helmfile apply -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**Option B: WVA-only** (connect to existing deployment):
```bash
export LLMD_NAMESPACE=llm-d-inference-scheduler
export LLMD_RELEASE_NAME_POSTFIX=inference-scheduling
export WVA_NAMESPACE=llm-d-autoscaler
cd guides/workload-autoscaling
helmfile apply -e wva-only -n ${WVA_NAMESPACE}
```

steps.title: Install Prometheus Adapter
steps.description:
Install Prometheus Adapter **after** WVA (which creates the required `prometheus-ca` ConfigMap):

```bash
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
export MON_NS=${MON_NS:-llm-d-monitoring}

curl -o ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/prometheus-adapter-values.yaml \
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d-incubation/workload-variant-autoscaler/v0.5.0/config/samples/prometheus-adapter-values.yaml

helm upgrade -i prometheus-adapter prometheus-community/prometheus-adapter \
  --version 5.2.0 -n ${MON_NS} --create-namespace \
  -f ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/prometheus-adapter-values.yaml
```

See the guide for OpenShift-specific and Kind/HTTPS-specific adapter configuration.

steps.title: Verify end-to-end installation

resolution.summary: A successful installation shows the WVA controller, Prometheus adapter, and HPA all running. The `inferno_desired_replicas` external metric should be available, and the HPA should be scaling the inference deployment based on saturation metrics.

uninstall.title: Remove WVA stack
uninstall.description:
**Full installation:**
```bash
cd guides/workload-autoscaling
helmfile destroy -n ${NAMESPACE:-llm-d-autoscaler}
```

**WVA-only:**
```bash
helmfile destroy -e wva-only -n ${NAMESPACE:-llm-d-autoscaler}
```

uninstall.title: Remove Prometheus Adapter
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall prometheus-adapter -n ${MON_NS:-llm-d-monitoring}
```

The llm-d stack continues operating without autoscaling after WVA removal.

upgrade.title: Check for breaking changes
upgrade.description:
**v0.5.0 breaking change:** The `scaleTargetRef` field is now required in VariantAutoscaling CRD. Existing resources must be updated before upgrading.

Check existing resources:
```bash
kubectl get variantautoscalings -A
```

upgrade.title: Update existing VariantAutoscaling resources
upgrade.description:
Add the required `scaleTargetRef` to each resource:
```bash
kubectl edit variantautoscaling <name> -n <namespace>
```

Add:
```yaml
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    kind: Deployment
    name: <your-deployment-name>
```

troubleshooting.title: WVA cannot connect to Prometheus
troubleshooting.description:
WVA requires HTTPS for Prometheus connections. Verify Prometheus is accessible with TLS:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=workload-variant-autoscaler -n ${NAMESPACE} --tail=50
```

For Kind/Minikube, ensure TLS is configured on Prometheus (see Step 2 in the guide).

troubleshooting.title: External metrics not available
troubleshooting.description:
If `inferno_desired_replicas` metric is not found:
```bash
kubectl get --raw "/apis/external.metrics.k8s.io/v1beta1" | jq
```

Verify Prometheus Adapter is running and configured correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n ${MON_NS} -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus-adapter
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus-adapter -n ${MON_NS} --tail=50
```

troubleshooting.title: HPA not scaling
troubleshooting.description:
Check HPA status and events:
```bash
kubectl describe hpa -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Verify the VariantAutoscaling resource has a valid `scaleTargetRef` (required in v0.5.0+).

troubleshooting.title: Scale-to-zero not working
troubleshooting.description: VariantAutoscalings without `scaleTargetRef` cannot scale to zero even with HPAScaleToZero enabled. Update the resource to include the target deployment reference.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster (v1.31+) with GPU support, Prometheus with HTTPS, Gateway API with Istio, and either an existing llm-d Inference Scheduling deployment or GPU resources for a fresh full installation.
